title: adenocalymma allamandiflorum
intro: Adenocalymma allamandiflorum, commonly known as the "Allamanda", is a flowering plant species in the family Apocynaceae. It is a popular ornamental plant, known for its bright yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Adenocalymma allamandiflorum include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against oxidative stress and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been reported to exhibit therapeutic actions such as analgesic, antispasmodic, and antiseptic activities.

parts: Adenocalymma allamandiflorum has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and flowers, which are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Adenocalymma allamandiflorum are known to contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant contain alkaloids, including adenocalycin and allamandin, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antitumor activities. The flowers of Adenocalymma allamandiflorum are a source of iridoid glycosides, including adenocalyxin and allamandigenin, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
preparations: Adenocalymma allamandiflorum has many herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures. side_effects: Adenocalymma allamandiflorum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals.
